title: Verify email — valid token: loading state then 'Email verified!' and Go to Login button

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.
Backend is running with a working GET /auth/verify-email endpoint.

A valid, unexpired verification token must be available in DATA-1. Obtain by: (1) creating a new account via POST /auth/signup, (2) retrieving the token from Mailpit at http://localhost:8025, or (3) obtaining the token directly from the backend DB for local testing.
FE-BE-GAP-028: Ensure MOBILE_DEEPLINK_SCHEME=companion:// is set in .env so backend generates correct scheme. For web tests, the token is passed as a URL query param, not a deep link.

testData.name: validToken
testData.description: A valid unexpired email verification token. Must be obtained from the backend (e.g., via Mailpit or DB query) as part of test setup. Cannot be generated client-side.

journey.description: Navigate to verify-email screen with a valid token query parameter. On web, Expo Router reads the token from the URL via useLocalSearchParams.
Loading state is shown while the API call is in flight.

target.text: Email verified! You can now log in.

journey.description: Success state text appears after API call succeeds.
router.replace to login after tapping Go to Login.

assertions.description: Loading state is visible while API call is pending.
Verify email API call is made on mount.

target.text: Email verified! You can now log in.

assertions.description: Success status renders the verified message.
Go to Login button is visible in success state.
Tapping Go to Login navigates to login screen.

manualValidationRequired.reason: Simulator deep link testing (xcrun simctl openurl) is a manual step described in the milestone implementation doc. Not automated in the current web test framework.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: The token must be obtained from the backend before running this test. Options: (1) retrieve from Mailpit at http://localhost:8025 after signup, (2) query the database directly, (3) use a backend admin endpoint to generate a token.
On web, the token is passed as a URL query param: /(auth)/verify-email?token=<value>. Expo Router's useLocalSearchParams reads it from the URL.
FE-BE-GAP-028 affects deep link scheme on native devices only. Web URL routing is unaffected.
The loading state ('Verifying your email...') may be very brief. If the API is fast, the validator may need to observe it via a fast network intercept rather than timing-based assertion.
